title: Voluntary Consumer AI Disclosure Pilot Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Voluntary Consumer AI Disclosure Pilot Act is a law that proposes a pilot scheme for companies to disclose information about the AI they use in consumer-facing products and services. The goal of this law is to increase transparency and potentially enhance trust among consumers.

title: Protecting Families from AI Data Center Energy Costs Act
action: Forwarded by Subcommittee to Full Committee (Amended) by Voice Vote.
summary: The Protecting Families from AI Data Center Energy Costs Act aims to safeguard families from the financial burdens of energy costs associated with Artificial Intelligence data centers. The purpose of this act is to protect everyday families from being financially affected by AI data centers.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Environment and Public Works.
summary: This document likely outlines a proposed United States federal law to regulate and address the environmental impacts caused by Artificial Intelligence. The Act's primary goal is to mitigate the potential negative effects of AI on ecosystems and biodiversity.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, and in addition to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026 aims to address the potential environmental effects of artificial intelligence. This legislation likely seeks to regulate or mitigate any negative environmental consequences related to the development and use of artificial intelligence.

title: To require certain artificial intelligence model developers to submit reports to the Secretary of Commerce, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: This document aims to require certain artificial intelligence model developers to submit reports to the Secretary of Commerce. The policy is intended to achieve other purposes as well, but this information is not specified in the title.

title: To direct the Comptroller General of the United States to conduct a study on the impacts of artificial intelligence on the workforce in the United States, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Education and Workforce.
summary: This document instructs the Comptroller General to research how artificial intelligence affects workers in the US. The study will also cover additional unspecified purposes.

title: Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Foreign Affairs, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act is a policy aimed at putting a temporary halt on the development and construction of artificial intelligence data centers. The specifics of this policy, including its scope, implementation, and goals, are currently unknown due to the lack of a provided description.

title: Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on the Judiciary, and in addition to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026 is a proposed law that aims to establish regulations and guidelines for the development and use of artificial intelligence across various sectors. The act seeks to balance innovation and safety by providing a framework for AI governance.

title: Algorithmic Accountability Act of 2025
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: The Algorithmic Accountability Act of 2025 is a legislative document that aims to establish accountability for algorithms and artificial intelligence systems used by federal agencies. Its purpose is to ensure transparency, fairness, and reliability in these AI systems.

title: Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2026 is a policy aimed at managing risks associated with the use of artificial intelligence. It likely establishes guidelines and regulations to ensure the safe and responsible development and deployment of AI systems.

title: SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026
action: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Small Business and Entrepreneurship.
summary: The SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026 aims to outline the use of artificial intelligence within the Small Business Administration. The exact details are not available as a description is not provided.

title: A bill to address the effects of artificial intelligence-enabled systems, including artificial intelligence chatbots, on older adults, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
summary: This bill aims to address the impact of artificial intelligence-enabled systems, including chatbots, on older adults. The legislation is intended to address "other purposes" related to AI's effects.

title: Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 619.
summary: The Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act aims to create a regulatory framework that allows for the development and use of artificial intelligence in the financial sector. The goal is to balance innovation with safety and security, enabling the growth of AI technologies while protecting consumers and financial institutions.

title: China AI Power Report Act
action: Ordered to be Reported (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 47 - 0.
summary: The China AI Power Report Act is a policy document that deals with the development and regulation of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in China, likely aiming to enhance the country's AI capabilities and technological advancements.

title: Responsible Artificial Intelligence Defense Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services.
summary: The Responsible Artificial Intelligence Defense Act of 2026 is a proposed legislation aimed at regulating the development and use of artificial intelligence to promote safety and security. The exact details of the policy are not available, but it may seek to establish guidelines and standards for AI development and deployment.

title: READ AI Models Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The READ AI Models Act is a legislation that regulates the creation and use of artificial intelligence models, requiring developers to ensure their models are reliable and transparent.

title: Workforce for AI Trust Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Workforce for AI Trust Act is a policy aimed at establishing trust in AI systems by addressing the workforce aspects of their development and deployment. However, without further information, the exact scope and details of this act are unknown.

title: K–12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Education and Workforce.
summary: This policy document, the K-12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026, aims to improve education for students in grades K-12 by making them literate and prepared for a society that increasingly uses artificial intelligence. The act likely focuses on introducing AI education and skills in schools to benefit students in their future careers and daily lives.

title: Preventing AI Censorship Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: The Preventing AI Censorship Act is a proposed law that aims to prevent AI systems from censoring or limiting the spread of information. The details of how this act would be executed are not specified in the title.

title: Generative AI Terrorism Risk Assessment Act
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs.
summary: This document likely outlines an assessment of the potential risks and threats posed by generative AI, focusing on its potential use in acts of terrorism. It may provide guidelines or regulations for mitigating these risks and ensuring the safe development and use of generative AI technologies.

title: Department of Advanced Technology and Artificial Intelligence Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.
summary: The Department of Advanced Technology and Artificial Intelligence Act is a proposed law that focuses on the department responsible for overseeing and regulating advanced technology and artificial intelligence. The details of the act are not provided, but it likely includes provisions for governance and regulation in this area.

title: Fraudulent Artificial Intelligence Regulations (FAIR) Elections Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Rules and Administration.
summary: The Fraudulent Artificial Intelligence Regulations (FAIR) Elections Act of 2026 is a law aimed at preventing the misuse of AI in elections. This law likely seeks to establish rules and guidelines to maintain the integrity and security of elections in the face of potential artificial intelligence threats.

title: A bill to prohibit certain uses of automated decision systems by employers, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
summary: This bill aims to restrict how employers use automated decision systems, likely involving job applications and employee evaluations. The specific details are unclear due to the lack of a description, but it seems to address fairness and transparency concerns in the workplace.

title: A bill to amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to impose an excise tax on systemically important AI activity, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance.
summary: This bill aims to introduce an excise tax on certain AI activities that are deemed systemically important. The tax is intended for "other purposes" although the specific details are not provided.

title: Secure and Accountable Military AI Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services.
summary: The Secure and Accountable Military AI Act of 2026 is a policy aiming to regulate the use of military artificial intelligence. The goal is likely to balance the benefits of AI in the military with the need for security and accountability.

title: Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act is a proposed law that aims to shield consumers from misleading or dishonest artificial intelligence. The law likely targets fake online reviews, biased algorithms, and other forms of deceptive AI practices that could manipulate or deceive consumers.

title: Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act aims to safeguard consumers from deceitful artificial intelligence practices. This act likely establishes laws and regulations to prevent misleading AI-generated information and maintain transparency in AI usage.

title: Heat Emergency Assessment and Tracking using AI Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: This AI policy document outlines a law to utilize artificial intelligence in assessing and tracking heat emergencies. The purpose of the law is to improve heat-related crisis response and management through AI technology.
